opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: The lump on my back suddenly burst, it's bleeding everywhere, and the pain is keeping me awake.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: A lump I've had on my upper back for months burst about three hours ago tonight and now it's bleeding, oozing, and so sore I can't lie down or sleep — I live on my own and can't reach it to clean or dress it.

symptom_narrative.onset: the lump has been there for months but got bigger and angrier over the last two days; it burst on its own about three hours ago
symptom_narrative.duration: about 3 hours since it burst; the swelling has been worsening over the past 48 hours
symptom_narrative.character: a firm lump under the skin that swelled up, then split open and started leaking blood and thick yellowish fluid; the skin around it is red and very tender
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the pain is severe and constant — it's keeping me awake and I can't lie flat on my back
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: on my upper back, where I can't really see or reach it
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant since it burst, worse with any pressure or when clothing rubs on it
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing much — sitting forward and keeping pressure off it helps a little
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: lying on my back, and my shirt rubbing against it
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: bleeding that keeps coming back and yellowish discharge; no shivering or shaking that I've noticed
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: the lump has been there a long time and a doctor once told me it was nothing to worry about, but it's never burst or been this sore before
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: can't sleep, can't lie on my back, and I can't reach it to clean or dress it myself

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: a longstanding lump on the upper back, previously said to be harmless

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives alone; unable to reach or dress the lesion on the back without help

objective_data_offered.value: a lump on the upper back that has split open in the last few hours; skin around it looks red and angry, it's oozing blood-stained fluid and thick yellowish discharge, with crusting at the centre
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: patient cannot easily see or photograph their own posterior upper back; view may be partial and off-angle

objective_data_offered.value: hasn't taken a temperature; says the skin around the lump feels warm but denies fevers, shivering or shaking chills
blood-stained bedsheets; bleeding has been slow and keeps restarting rather than gushing
